hero.headline: Builds things that ship.
hero.subline: Full-stack products, native apps, and the infrastructure under them — taken from idea to something real people use.
hero.kicker: Niranjan Rakesh — engineer, based in the UAE

about.lead: I build complete things. Not demos, not prototypes that die in a folder — products with users, uptime, and edges that have been sanded by real use.
body: Trained in Computer Science, comfortable at the layer below the framework: I've shipped a production backend with zero npm dependencies, hand-rolled drag-and-drop from raw pointer events, and tuned video sync algorithms for connections that don't behave.

The through-line is judgment about how things should feel — inputs that never bounce, apps that boot straight into the point, tools that meet you where you already are. Design was my first language; engineering is how I make it real.

themes.title: Ships complete things
themes.detail: Idea → product → production infrastructure, end to end, solo.

themes.title: Depth over glue
themes.detail: Zero-dependency servers, custom sync logic, raw pointer events — building below the framework when it's better.

themes.title: Product judgment
themes.detail: Decisions driven by how the thing feels to use, not by what's easiest to wire together.

smallerBuilds.name: Mac Transcoder
smallerBuilds.line: Native macOS drag-and-drop video transcoder using hardware VideoToolbox encoding — MP4s out, zero settings.

smallerBuilds.name: PerfectTasks
smallerBuilds.line: Full-stack task manager (Next.js, Drizzle, Clerk) designed, planned, and built test-first in a single day.

smallerBuilds.name: orbit-core
smallerBuilds.line: NestJS + Prisma backend for time management — OAuth, Google Calendar sync, multi-device conflict resolution, BullMQ jobs.

footer.line: Designed and built by Niranjan Rakesh — no templates, no site builder.
